ENGAA-2022-M1-001: Which one of the following is a simplification of $y\left(\dfrac{3x^{1/2}z}{y^3}\right)^2$?
ENGAA-2022-PHY-001: There is a constant current in a conducting wire. A charge of 20 C passes through the wire in 1.5 minutes. An 18 cm straight section of this wire lies in a uniform magnetic field, perpendicular to the direction of the field. The magnetic field strength is 0.15 T. What is the magnitude of the magnetic force on this section of wire?
ENGAA-2022-M1-002: Find the complete set of values of $x$ that satisfy the inequality $\dfrac34(5-x) - \dfrac12(6-x) - x < 0$.
ENGAA-2022-PHY-002: Two identical resistors are connected in parallel to a 6.0 V battery. The two resistors dissipate a total power of 0.15 W. One of these resistors is removed from the circuit and connected to a 12 V battery. How much charge passes through this resistor in 6.0 minutes?
ENGAA-2022-M1-003: Rob keeps a record of what he earns each day. On Monday, he earned 50% less than he earned on Sunday. On Tuesday, he earned 20% more than he earned on Monday. On Wednesday, he earned 30% less than he earned on Tuesday. On Wednesday, he earned £84. How much did Rob earn on Sunday?
ENGAA-2022-PHY-003: Ultrasound is used to find a crack inside a cuboid block of metal. An ultrasound probe is held in contact with the top surface, perpendicular to it. A short pulse is sent at $t=0$ ms and reflects from both the crack and the bottom surface. [DIAGRAM: graph of reflected pulse strength vs time, showing two peaks between 0 and 0.3 ms, and a diagram of the probe on the metal block with an internal crack]. The speed of ultrasound in the metal is 5000 m/s. What is the distance between the bottom surface of the metal block and the crack?
ENGAA-2022-M1-004: Which one of the following is a simplification of $\dfrac{5x^2-17x-12}{25x^2-9} \div \dfrac{x^2+x-12}{x^2-x-6}$?
ENGAA-2022-PHY-004: Power is supplied to an electric motor at 0.800 kW. The motor has an efficiency of 60% and is switched on for half an hour. How much energy is wasted during this time?
ENGAA-2022-M1-005: A rectangle PQRS has length $(2x-1)$ cm and width $(x+1)$ cm. A larger rectangle is made by adding 3 cm to both the length and the width of PQRS. The larger rectangle has an area of $360\,\text{cm}^2$. What is the ratio of PQ to PS?
ENGAA-2022-PHY-005: The graph shows potential difference plotted against current for a filament lamp and a resistor. [DIAGRAM: I-V graph, filament lamp curve (bending) and resistor line (straight through origin), pd axis 0-10V, current axis 0-0.4A]. The lamp and resistor are connected in parallel to a 6.0 V power supply and the current in the lamp, $I$, is recorded. In a second circuit, the lamp and resistor are connected in series to the same power supply, and the current in the resistor is 0.18 A. The potential difference across the lamp, $V$, is recorded. What are the values of $I$ in the first circuit and $V$ in the second circuit?
ENGAA-2022-M1-006: PQRS is a trapezium. Angle P = 30°, PQ = 40 m, and $\tan(\angle RSQ) = \dfrac58$. [DIAGRAM: trapezium PQRS with QR parallel to PS, angle at P = 30°, diagonal SQ shown]. What is the length of PS, in metres?
ENGAA-2022-PHY-006: A transverse wave on a string has a speed of 500 m/s. The horizontal distance between two points P and Q on the wave, which are one wavelength apart, is 4.0 m. [DIAGRAM: wave snapshot showing points P, X, Q with X at the crest, 6.0 mm amplitude]. At time $t=0$ ms, point X on the string is at its maximum displacement of 6.0 mm above equilibrium. What is the displacement of point X at time $t=7.0$ ms?
ENGAA-2022-M1-007: A solid cylinder has radius $r$ cm and height $h$ cm. A cube has side length $3r$ cm. The total surface area of the cylinder is equal to four times the total surface area of the cube. Which of the following is an expression for $h$ in terms of $r$?
ENGAA-2022-PHY-007: A piece of metal of mass 50 g is at thermal equilibrium in a hot liquid at temperature $T$. The metal is removed and immediately placed in 100 g of water at 20°C. The water is stirred and reaches a final temperature of 26°C. Specific heat capacities: hot liquid 2000 J/(kg°C), metal 350 J/(kg°C), water 4200 J/(kg°C). What is the temperature $T$ of the hot liquid? (Assume heat transfers to surroundings are negligible.)
ENGAA-2022-M1-008: The variables $x$ and $y$ are related by the equation $x = 5 - \dfrac{2y^3+1}{1-2y^3}$. Which of the following is a rearrangement to make $y$ the subject?
ENGAA-2022-PHY-008: A bar magnet is placed at position X close to one end of a coil, on the axis of the coil. [DIAGRAM: coil with bar magnet, velocity-time graph showing the magnet moving from X to Y and back to X]. The graph shows how the velocity of the magnet varies as it moves rapidly to position Y and back to position X. The magnetic field of the bar magnet still affects the coil when at position Y. Which graph represents how the induced voltage in the coil changes as the magnet moves?
ENGAA-2022-M1-009: Three different numbers are chosen at random from 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. What is the probability that the three numbers form the three sides of a right-angled triangle?
ENGAA-2022-PHY-009: A small slider of mass 30 g is at rest near the bottom of a frictionless slope, in contact with a light uncompressed spring. The spring is compressed by 5.0 cm and the slider remains in contact with it. The spring is released and causes the slider to rise up the slope to a maximum vertical height of 20 cm. The slider is replaced with one of mass 20 g. The spring is now compressed by 15 cm, and the new slider remains in contact with it. To what maximum vertical height does this new slider rise after release? (The spring obeys Hooke's law; assume air resistance is negligible.)
ENGAA-2022-M1-010: The point $(-1, 5)$ is translated to the point $(3, 2)$ by two successive translations. The first translation is by the vector $\begin{pmatrix}3p\\-4p\end{pmatrix}$ and the second is by the vector $\begin{pmatrix}q\\-2q\end{pmatrix}$. What is the value of $p+q$?
ENGAA-2022-PHY-010: A tall, smooth cylinder contains air at atmospheric pressure of $1.00\times10^5\,\text{Pa}$. The density of the air in the cylinder is $1.20\,\text{kg/m}^3$. A heavy piston is now placed in the top of the cylinder and allowed to fall slowly downwards, compressing the air until it rests in equilibrium. The mass of the piston is 50.0 kg and its cross-sectional area is $0.0200\,\text{m}^2$. What is the density of the air in the cylinder when the piston rests in equilibrium? (g = 10 N/kg; assume ideal gas, constant temperature.)
ENGAA-2022-PHY-011: The diagram represents a stationary wave in a medium spanning 150 cm, showing three complete loops (three full waves) along its length. [DIAGRAM: stationary wave pattern over 150 cm]. The transverse waves creating the stationary wave travel at a speed of 300 m/s through the medium. What is the frequency of the transverse waves?
ENGAA-2022-M2-002: The diagram shows a semicircle of radius 5 units centred at $(5,0)$, spanning from $(0,0)$ to $(10,0)$. A triangle has vertices at $(0,0)$, $(10,0)$ and $(x,y)$, where $(x,y)$ is a point on the arc of the semicircle. Which of the following is an expression in terms of $x$ for the area of this triangle?
ENGAA-2022-PHY-012: A stone is thrown vertically upwards from the surface of the Earth and reaches a maximum height $h$. The same stone is thrown vertically upwards from the surface of the Moon, with the same initial speed. What is the maximum height reached on the Moon? (g on Earth = 10 N/kg; g on Moon = 1.6 N/kg; ignore air resistance.)
ENGAA-2022-M2-003: Four mathematically similar solids, W, X, Y and Z, have the following properties: the ratio of the lengths of W to X is 1:2; the ratio of the surface area of X to Y is 2:1; the ratio of the volume of Y to Z is 1:2. What is the order of the solids when arranged in increasing volume?
ENGAA-2022-PHY-013: A ray of light is incident on a boundary between a vacuum and medium X at angle $\theta$ to the normal. [DIAGRAM: incident ray, reflected ray, and refracted ray at a vacuum-medium X boundary]. The incident ray is partially reflected and partially refracted. The angle between the reflected and refracted rays is 90°. What is the refractive index of medium X?
ENGAA-2022-M2-004: Which one of the following expressions is equal to $\dfrac{(2+\sqrt{20})^2}{(1+\sqrt5)^3}$?
ENGAA-2022-PHY-014: An electric train travels along a straight horizontal track, passing point Q at $t=0$. Its distance from Q is given by $x = at + bt^2$, where $a$ and $b$ are constants. Which of the following statements is/are correct? 1: The speed of the train increases with time at a constant rate. 2: The resultant force acting on the train increases with time. 3: The rate at which energy is transferred to the train increases with time.
ENGAA-2022-PHY-015: A length of wire has resistance $R$. Another length of wire is made from the same material, twice as long, with half the diameter. Both wires have circular cross-sections. The two wires are connected in parallel. What is the total resistance of this combination?
ENGAA-2022-M2-006: What is the complete set of real values of $x$ for which $x^2(x^2+4) < 21$?
ENGAA-2022-PHY-016: An empty bucket has a mass of 1.20 kg and internal volume of $0.0150\,\text{m}^3$. The bucket is used to lift water from a well, attached to a rope winding onto a rotating cylinder of radius 0.200 m turned by a handle. To lift a bucket completely full of water at constant speed, a force of 250 N must be applied to the handle, acting tangent to the circle of radius 0.600 m in which the handle moves. The energy required to lift the (empty) bucket is wasted energy. What is the efficiency of the system in lifting water from the well? (g = 10 N/kg; density of water = 1000 kg/m³.)
ENGAA-2022-M2-007: A sector of a circle has perimeter 24. For what value of the radius does the sector have the maximum possible area?
ENGAA-2022-PHY-017: A uniform metre ruler has a mass of 100 g and is 1.00 m long. A small object of mass 20 g is fixed at the 0 cm mark, and another of mass 80 g at the 100 cm mark. The ruler is balanced on a pivot. When balanced, what distance is the pivot from the 0 cm mark?
ENGAA-2022-M2-008: The curve $y=x^2-x-6$ intersects the x-axis at points A and B, and has a minimum at point C. The rectangle ABDE has two of its vertices at A and B, and C lies on edge DE, between D and E. What is the area of the rectangle ABDE?
ENGAA-2022-PHY-018: A circuit comprising two resistors (3.0 Ω and 1.0 Ω) and two batteries (3.0 V and 6.0 V), with negligible internal resistance, is set up as shown, with junctions X and Y. [DIAGRAM: circuit with 3.0 Ω and 1.0 Ω resistors and 3.0 V, 6.0 V batteries between junctions X and Y]. What are the magnitude and direction of the current in the 3.0 V battery?
ENGAA-2022-M2-009: A straight line passes through the points $(0, 2a)$ and $(a, 0)$, where $a$ is a positive constant. What is the perpendicular distance of the point $P(a, 2a)$ from this line?
ENGAA-2022-PHY-019: Two isolated spheres have masses $m$ and $2m$. They move towards each other along the same line with speeds $4v$ and $v$ respectively, then collide and coalesce. What is the loss of kinetic energy during the collision?
ENGAA-2022-M2-010: Find the sum of the real solutions to the equation $2^x - \left(\sqrt2\right)^{x+6} + 12 = 0$.
ENGAA-2022-PHY-020: A steel cable has mass 64 kg and cross-sectional area $2.0\times10^{-4}\,\text{m}^2$. The Young modulus of steel is $2.0\times10^{11}\,\text{Pa}$. When the cable lies on horizontal ground its length is 40 m. What is its extension when it is suspended freely from one end and hangs vertically? (g = 10 N/kg; assume the cable obeys Hooke's law.)
